growth_records.strain: Anabaena sp. PCC 7120; Anabaena cylindrica ATCC29414; Anabaena borealis; Scytonema hofmanni PCC 7110; Leptolyngbya sp. (desert crust); Phormidium persicinum; Trichodesmium erythraeum; Nodularia spumigena; Chroococcidiopsis sp. PCC 9317; Microcystis aeruginosa PCC 7806 (group)

up.method: 13C stable-isotope labeling (δ13C-CH4); carbon source but net inorganic-C uptake rate not quantified

sec.method: MIMS continuous measurement over 2-5 days; production rate from slope of CH4 profile normalized to cyanobacterial dry weight, corrected for continuous degassing from semi-open chamber

growth_records.cite: Bižić M. et al. Aquatic and terrestrial cyanobacteria produce methane. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aax5343. [PMC6962044]
growth_records.snippet: Hourly CH4 production rates across cultures of larger cyanobacteria were in the range of 0.1 to 3.4 μmol g−1 hour−1 in individual experiments and a mean of 0.51 ± 0.26 μmol g−1 hour−1.
growth_records.notes: Aggregate secretion rate reported for the group of 'larger cyanobacteria' (per-strain values only in Fig. 3, not in text). CH4 secretion rate = 0.51 ± 0.26 µmol CH4 (g dry weight)^-1 h^-1, individual-experiment range 0.1-3.4 µmol g^-1 h^-1. No growth rate (mu) reported: study measured CH4 production, not growth. No pH given; media BG11/f/2/YBCII cited by reference, compositions not stated (BG11 P content noted as ca. 200 µmol/L in Discussion). Temperatures per Table 1: BG11 strains 30°C, Phormidium & Trichodesmium 26°C, Nodularia 20°C — group temperature set to the modal 30°C. Carbon source NaH13CO3 (13C-bicarbonate); inorganic-C uptake demonstrated by 13C incorporation into CH4 but not quantified as a rate.
